notes: Create-heavy timing/effect plans should use /xlightsdesigner/api/sequencing/apply-batch-plan with the expected revision token.
Supported direct owned API commands may apply explicit effect/layer/display-order edits that are not represented by the batch endpoint.
Unsupported command graphs must fail closed instead of falling back to transaction or legacy automation endpoints.
Post-apply verification reads back revision state, timing marks, effects windows, and render evidence when available.
Sequence-level rendering controls are planner context unless the owned API exposes explicit sequence settings mutation.
